FULL CONTENT:
# Research on the Impact of Competency-Based Curriculum Design on Student Learning Outcomes

## Authors

## DOI:

## Abstract

With the continuous changes in the educational environment, traditional knowledge-oriented curricula can no longer adequately meet students' practical competency needs in modern society. Consequently, competency-based curriculum design has gradually emerged as a key method for enhancing educational quality and students' practical abilities. This paper explores the impact of competency-based curriculum design on student learning outcomes, analyzes the theoretical framework of competency-based education, including constructivist learning theory and social cognitive theory, proposes a theoretical model and key elements of competency-based curriculum design, and discusses implementation strategies and resource support systems. The study finds that competency-based curriculum design significantly enhances students' knowledge mastery, skill application, and learning attitudes through clear competency goals, task-driven teaching strategies, and effective assessment systems. However, the dynamic adjustment of curriculum design remains crucial for improving student learning outcomes. Future research should focus on optimizing the adjustment mechanisms and assessment methods of curriculum design to further enhance educational quality and students' practical abilities.

![](https://www.atlantic-press-journals.com/public/journals/3/cover_issue_15_en_US.jpg)

![](https://www.journal.ypidathu.or.id/public/journals/10/pageHeaderLogoImage_en_US.png)

# The Impact of Competency-Based Pedagogy on Students’ Motivation and Engagement: An Experimental Study

![](https://crossmark-cdn.crossref.org/widget/v2.0/logos/CROSSMARK_Color_horizontal.svg)

## Article Sidebar

![](https://www.journal.ypidathu.or.id/public/journals/10/cover_issue_123_en_US.png)

## Abstract

ABSTRACT  
Background. Competency-based pedagogy (CBP) has emerged as a transformative instructional approach aligned with 21st-century educational demands, emphasizing the mastery of specific skills and learner autonomy. Despite its increasing adoption, empirical evidence regarding its impact on student motivation and engagement remains limited, especially in higher education contexts.   
Purpose. This study investigates the impact of CBP on undergraduate students’ motivation and classroom engagement in Indonesia.  
Method. Using a quasi-experimental pretest-posttest control group design, 120 students were assigned to experimental and control conditions. The CBP group experienced mastery learning, self-paced modules, and formative feedback, while the control group followed conventional lecture methods. Data were collected using validated scales and analyzed with ANCOVA.   
Results. The results showed significant increases in both motivation and engagement in the CBP group (p < 0.001), with large effect sizes. This study provides empirical validation for the integration of CBP in higher education and highlights its role in enhancing learner autonomy and affective outcomes.  
Conclusion.This study concludes that competency-based pedagogy positively influences students’ motivational and engagement levels, supporting its integration into curriculum design to foster meaningful and active learning experiences.

How Competency-Based Education is Improving Student Outcomes
============================================================

Marzano Academies – Competency Based Education

![Image 3: How Competency-Based Education is Improving Student Outcomes](https://marzanoacademies.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/How-Competency-Based-Education-is-Improving-Student-Outcomes.webp)

Competency-based education (CBE) is revolutionizing the way school districts and educators approach teaching and learning. By focusing on mastery of specific competencies or skills rather than time spent in a classroom, CBE allows for personalized learning paths that are tailored to the needs, pace, and interests of individual students. This shift from a one-size-fits-all model to a more individualized approach is showing promising outcomes in various educational settings.

At its core, CBE is about understanding that students learn in different ways and at different speeds. In a traditional education system, students’ progress through material based on age and grade levels, often moving forward based on the school calendar rather than their understanding of the material. CBE, on the other hand, ensures that a student moves on only after demonstrating a certain level of proficiency in a subject or skill.

One of the primary ways that CBE aids school districts is through its flexibility. In a competency-based system, curriculum can be designed to align with the specific competencies students need to master. This allows for the integration of cross-curricular projects, real-world problem solving, and critical thinking exercises that engage students in different ways. Schools can adopt diverse instructional strategies, from project-based learning to flipped classrooms, which can cater to different learning styles.

For teachers, CBE provides the structure to create detailed learning progressions and clear criteria for assessment. This clarity enables educators to identify precisely where a student may be struggling and offer targeted interventions. The granularity of data available in CBE allows teachers to customize instruction for each student, which is particularly beneficial for those who may need more support or for those who are ready to advance more quickly.

Moreover, competency-based education has the potential to close equity gaps. Students from diverse backgrounds, including those with special education needs or English language learners, benefit from the system’s adaptability. Teachers can modify content, process, and product based on individual student needs, providing equitable opportunities for all students to succeed.

Another advantage is the emphasis CBE places on real world skills. As the curriculum is tailored to individual students, it can also be aligned with the skills and knowledge that are in demand in the local community and job market. This helps to ensure that students are not only learning for academic success but are also preparing for future careers.

Furthermore, technology plays a pivotal role in CBE. Digital platforms and learning management systems enable teachers to track student progress on competencies in real time, providing immediate feedback and allowing for adjustments in the learning pathway as needed. These technologies also offer a wealth of resources for differentiated instruction, further enabling personalized learning experiences.

In conclusion, competency-based education supports school districts and teachers in creating a more adaptable, personalized, and relevant learning environment. By focusing on the mastery of competencies, CBE ensures that each student can succeed at their own pace, with curriculum that prepares them for both academic progression and the demands of the modern world. As educational paradigms shift to meet the diverse needs of students, CBE stands out as a promising approach for a more inclusive and effective educational system.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Competency-based curriculum design significantly enhances student knowledge mastery, skill application, and positive learning attitudes, leading to improved academic performance and engagement.
Faculty report that competency-based pedagogy fosters deeper student engagement and autonomy, allowing for tailored instructional strategies that meet diverse learning needs.
Institutions face challenges in implementing competency-based education due to resource limitations, resistance to change, and the need for faculty training and curriculum alignment.
